key_drivers: Broad multi-session dollar weakness continues to support non-USD FX, precious metals, and equity index futures.
The dominant fresh macro change is a stronger crude backdrop, with a reported EIA inventory draw layered on top of ongoing Hormuz-related supply disruption risk.
Credit conditions remain supportive for equities while copper strength and improving China PMI data keep the Australian-dollar complex strongest in the basket.

stale_sources: Gold ETF flows reflect March 2026 monthly data published 2026-04-08
RBA stance relies on the latest official 2026-03-17 decision
ECB stance relies on the latest official 2026-03-19 decision
BoJ stance relies on the latest official 2026-03-19 decision
China growth relies on March 2026 PMI release
FRED DFII10 latest official observation is 2026-04-14
fallbacks_used: CNBC US10YTIP fallback for live real-yield confirmation was inaccessible, so real yields were left flat using official FRED

Current secondary coverage of the 2026-04-15 EIA release was used to confirm an approximately 0.9 million barrel crude draw because the official table was not directly parsed into the browser markdown
